description: Estimate prior probabilities from occurrence data, habitat, and spatial modeling

functions.name: build_priors
functions.description: High-level wrapper: GBIF fetch -> habitat -> grid -> model -> priors -> backbone translation

inputs.name: taxa
inputs.description: Data frame with taxonomy rank columns. Column names encode the rank (family, genus, species). IMPORTANT: the prior algorithm estimates sampling effort from multiple species captured by similar methods. A single target species is insufficient — provide a broader group (e.g. data.frame(family = 'Gobiidae') or a multi-species list) so the algorithm can gauge how well-sampled each grid cell is.

inputs.name: habitat_scheme
inputs.description: Habitat classification: NULL (default 3-category: Marine/Freshwater/Terrestrial), 'IUCN_L1' (18 IUCN Level 1 groups), or a custom data.frame with an l1_name column listing the habitat categories (e.g. data.frame(l1_name = c('Estuarine', 'Freshwater', 'Rocky Intertidal', 'Sandy Subtidal'))). If the user provides habitat names, construct a custom data frame.

inputs.name: target_backbone_id
inputs.description: Target backbone for translating prior taxon names (4 = NCBI, 11 = GBIF). Does not affect the GBIF occurrence query itself.

inputs.name: year_range
inputs.description: GBIF year filter as a comma-separated string, e.g. '2000,2024'. NULL (default) returns all years. Must be a string, NOT a numeric vector.

inputs.name: search_radius_deg
inputs.description: Radius in degrees around site for GBIF search. Larger values (5-6) provide more grid cells for the spatial model and produce much better species-habitat differentiation. Values below 3 often produce too few grid cells for the model to estimate species-specific habitat slopes.

output.description: List with $priors (data frame with grid_id, main_habitat, taxon_name, theta) and attributes

functions.scaling_note: GBIF + LLM + modeling; 10-60 min depending on taxon count and geographic scope

functions.name: optimize_grid_size
functions.description: Find optimal spatial grid resolution for prior estimation
